meta.siteDescription: Portfolio of Dennis Hawran — Gameplay Programmer, freshly minted Master in Game Development.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: PLAYER PROFILE
paragraphs: Hey, I'm Dennis — Gameplay Programmer with a fresh Master's in Game Development.

I build systems that feel good to play. Twin-stick combat, tower defense, controller-less VR, AR multiplayer, ML-driven Pong paddles — if it's playable, I want to know how it ticks. Seven years with Unity, plus Unreal, FUDGE, C++ and an unhealthy love for game jams.

My Master's project *Unthroned* taught me what I enjoy most: building the invisible. The systems under the hood that let the player *just play*. Before this I programmed and repaired production machines — problem-solving has been a professional condition longer than an academic one.
Now I'm looking for a team that takes games seriously without losing the fun. If that sounds like you: the coffee's on.

descriptions.unthroned:
A twin-stick shooter with base-building and tower-defense elements. Built as a team for our Master's degree. You play Dave, a dethroned necromancer reclaiming his brother's empire — farm souls, upgrade your base, send your army to raid.

My focus: core combat, enemy AI state machines, the weapon and upgrade system, and performance for the particle hordes.
descriptions.oasis:
A VR bachelor's thesis built for a haptic VR room — no controllers; real physical objects stand in for virtual ones, the player picks them up, and that drives the interaction. The goal was maximum immersion through 1:1 mapping of physical and virtual worlds.

My focus: the interaction system linking tracked real objects to VR counterparts, plus the Unreal Blueprint logic for the room's puzzles.
descriptions.aurea:
A low-poly AR multiplayer game for smartphones. Unity AR Foundation turns your living room into an arena — models in Blender and Maya, netcode for small matches, playful vibe.

My focus: AR plane detection, multiplayer sync via a relay server, and the gameplay feel.
descriptions.cozyWinter:
A cozy Christmas-market builder. No fail state, no deadline — just you, a blanket and some mulled wine. Place buildings, add decorations, watch the customers roll in.

Built during a game jam as an antidote to all stress games. I did nearly everything: code, design, balance.
descriptions.pumpkinHarvest:
A relaxed farming game. Plant, water, harvest, deliver — or just take a walk in the woods. The suburb wants pumpkins; you deliver. Atmosphere-first, no shooter-adrenaline curve.

Game-jam submission, built solo.
descriptions.witchWay:
Indirect control: you don't steer the witch, you shape the forest around her. Place objects, lay down arrows, keep her alive as long as you can. Every choice has side effects.

Student project in a game-jam format, team of three.
descriptions.gravityPong:
Pong — with gravity, and an ML agent as opponent. Trained with Unity ML-Agents, learns to steer the paddle itself. A small experiment where debugging the training was the real game.

Hobby project, fully solo.
descriptions.bomberman:
A Bomberman clone, built in the FUDGE engine at HFU. No prebuilt framework, every gameplay system from scratch.

Student project for the course "Development of Interactive Applications", team work focused on grid logic and bomb simulation.
descriptions.arcReactor:
3D model — Tony Stark's Arc Reactor, high-poly with PBR textures. Modeled in Blender, textured in Substance Painter.

Student project on game-asset creation.
descriptions.mjolnir:
3D model — Thor's hammer, high-poly, PBR textures with scratches and wear. Blender plus Substance Painter.

Student project on game-asset creation.
descriptions.coffeeExe:
Top-secret long-running project: reliably convert coffee into runnable code. Shipping since 1996, crashes without espresso, performance scales proportionally to bean strength.

Only known balance issue: excessive sugar introduces unexpected features. Pull requests (in bean form) welcome.
